title: Wrightia boranensis
taxonomy: Wrightia boranensis belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Within the family Apocynaceae, Wrightia boranensis is classified in the genus Wrightia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The genus Wrightia is further divided into several species, with Wrightia boranensis being one of them. Wrightia boranensis is a species of flowering plant that is native to the southwestern region of Thailand.

intro: Wrightia boranensis is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Wrightia or Nakhon Phanom tree. This plant has been traditionally used in Thai medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Wrightia boranensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Wrightia boranensis include tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to promote health and well-being in traditional Thai medicine.
